title: Incremental quantization ladder Q8->Q6->Q5 with +/-2pp stop rule (v4, judge-only)

bands.bar: delta >= -0.02, INCLUSIVE at exactly -0.02 (matches Phase 26 ci_lower >= -0.02, selection_v4.json)
bands.tie_rule: when two tiers' rho are exactly equal and both clear the bar, the LOWER tier ships -- the stop rule descends and ships the lowest tier within the band
bands.null_rule: a rung whose eval yields n_scored == 0 (all parse-fail) is status FAIL with judge_rho null -- never pending, never skipped, never a pass

stop_rule: Descend Q8->Q6->Q5. Ship the lowest tier whose judge rho is within 2pp of the Gate 1 baseline. HALT descent at the first tier that drops >2pp; do not measure or ship any lower tier after a halt.

ladder.interpretation_note: SUPERSEDED by the Q6 rung -- see ladder_q8.json's revised_interpretation block and this file's top-level noise_floor_finding. The -1.507pp delta is real (measured) but the 'Q8 is lossy / prune increased quantization sensitivity' READING of it is falsified: Q6 scored above both f16 and Q8, an ordering no degradation mechanism can produce.
This is the rung that falsifies the Q8 headline -- see top-level noise_floor_finding. Q6 scoring above f16 and above Q8 is not a real capability gain; it is sampling noise at n=121 single-seed.

reliability.note: Only tier in the ladder with a nonzero parse failure (n=120 of 121, the other three all n=121/parse_fail=0). See output/pkg-v4/ladder_q5.json's parse_fail_root_cause: a degenerate repetition-loop generation on index 109 that never closed before max_tokens. Weak evidence alone (1/121) but the only tier-discriminating signal available once rho is established as noise (see noise_floor_finding) -- disqualifies Q5 from the default/automatic ship pick despite its rho passing the band.

ladder.interpretation_note: rho passes the band (+0.584pp) but this is on an n=120 subset, not like-for-like with the other three n=121 rungs -- see ladder_q5.json measured.n_dataset_note. Per noise_floor_finding this rho is not meaningfully different from f16/Q8/Q6 either way.

noise_floor_finding.headline: Measured ordering is Q6 (0.8063) > f16 floor (0.8002) > Q8 (0.7851). A lossy compression of the f16 master (Q6_K or Q8_0) cannot legitimately carry MORE judge signal than its own uncompressed source -- Q6 scoring above f16 is not physically possible as a true quantization effect.
noise_floor_finding.conclusion: The rung-to-rung deltas (Q8 -1.51pp, Q6 +0.61pp, span 2.12pp) are well inside single-seed sampling noise at n=121 (95% CI half-widths ~7-8pp on every rung, including Gate 1 itself). f16, Q8, and Q6 are statistically indistinguishable on this measurement. This SUPERSEDES ladder_q8.json's original headline_finding ("Q8 is NOT lossless / the prune increased quantization sensitivity") -- that interpretation predicted monotonic decay (f16 > Q8 > Q6), and the measured ordering is the opposite. See ladder_q8.json's revised_interpretation and ladder_q6.json's noise_floor_finding for the full record.
noise_floor_finding.consequence_for_ship_selection: Because the rungs are statistically indistinguishable, ship_tier below cannot be read as 'the tier with the highest measured rho won' -- all passing tiers are equally supported by this evidence. Selection among them rests on size/robustness, not on which point estimate happened to be highest in this single run.

Q4-nf4.reason: MoE router-quant collapse: router weights quantized at the same uniform width as the experts; sparse routing cannot tolerate the blur. Tombstone: models/qwen3-30b-wp-30_70-merged-v2-4bit. PKG4-02 forbids uniform 4-bit nf4.

Q4-awq.reason: AWQ W4A16 is a vLLM artifact path, not a GGUF ship; judge-only GGUF ship does not need it.

ship_rationale: Q6_K ships. NOT because it has the highest measured rho (0.8063) -- noise_floor_finding above establishes that all four rungs (f16 0.8002, Q8 0.7851, Q6 0.8063, Q5 0.8060) are statistically indistinguishable, so 'highest rho' would be picking noise, not signal. The actual discriminator is reliability: f16/Q8/Q6 all scored parse_fail=0 at n=121; Q5_K_M is the only tier that produced a parse failure (1/121, a degenerate repetition-loop generation that never emitted a closing structured verdict -- see ladder_q5.json). For a judge model whose entire contract is a parseable rubric, that is a real functional defect, not a quality wobble, even though 1/121 is statistically weak evidence in isolation. Q6_K is the SMALLEST of the two zero-parse-failure tiers (23.47 GiB vs Q8's 30.37 GiB, ~23% smaller) at rho statistically tied with the f16 floor: measured judge_rho 0.8063205167262201, delta_vs_gate1 +0.006139656707505492 (+0.614pp), measured size 25200652096 bytes (23.4699 GiB). Ship rationale in one line: smallest artifact at statistically-equal measured quality with zero parse failures -- explicitly not 'highest rho won'.
deviation_from_literal_stop_rule: This is a DOCUMENTED, reasoned override of the plan's literal mechanical rule ('ship the lowest tier whose rho clears the band'), which taken literally and blind to reliability would select Q5 (rho passes at +0.584pp, and nothing measured below it). That literal rule was written assuming rho differences between tiers carry real signal; the ladder's own measurements (Q6 scoring above its own f16 source) falsified that assumption mid-execution (see noise_floor_finding). Once rho cannot discriminate, mechanically shipping 'the smallest tier that clears a noise-dominated band' would ship a checkpoint with a demonstrated generation-reliability defect (Q5's parse failure) over one with none (Q6), for a difference of ~3.1 GiB. This override is recorded here, in ladder_q5.json, and in 27-03-SUMMARY.md so a later reader is never misled about why Q6 (not Q5) ships. If this reasoning is not accepted, the honest fallback position is: Q5 and Q6 are both defensible and the choice between them (smaller-but-one-parse-failure vs slightly-larger-but-zero-failures) is a human call, not a mechanically-derivable one from this evidence alone.
downstream_note: Plan 27-04's card and Plan 27-05's manifest read ship_gguf as their single source. The shipped GGUF (any tier) carries the --no-mtp lineage from the f16 master -- no MTP/speculative-decoding head, per conversion_receipt_v4.json's mtp_deviation_note.
